{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Villemson Gambit ||rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|pd|pd|pd|pd||pd|pd|pd||||||||||||||||||||pl|pl|pd|||||||||||pl|pl|pl||||pl|pl|rl|nl|bl||kl||nl|rl|| |parent=[[Chess/King's Gambit Accepted|King’s Gambit Accepted]]}} =Villemson Gambit= ===3. d4=== Just like the Queen’s gambit, this move reveals an attack on the f4 pawn. However, the Villemson Gambit can cause black to wreak havoc with [[/3...Qh4/|Qh4+]]. White is forced to play [[/4. Ke2/|Ke2]] because if white plays [[/4. g3/|g3??]] black can respond fxg3 and the h2 pawn is pinned. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1.e4 e5 2.f4 exf4 3. d4'''
